gaps: No test case covers Poly Lens Cloud policy locking behavior for any device setting.
No test case covers Sensors & Presence settings UI for Voyager Free 20.
No test case validates that a locked (policy-enforced) setting displays the correct value in the app UI.
No test case verifies that a user cannot change a locked setting value (UI should show it as disabled/read-only).
No test case covers the interaction between Poly Lens Cloud remote policy management and the Poly Studio Mobile app's local UI rendering.
No test case validates the sync/refresh behavior when a cloud policy is applied while the app is already connected to the device.
No test case covers the Voyager Free 20 device-specific Sensors & Presence settings screen at all.
No lifecycle testing exists for policy-locked state: what happens when app is backgrounded/foregrounded after policy is applied, or when device disconnects/reconnects with an active policy.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-001: Verify Poly Lens Cloud locked policy displays correct value on Voyager Free 20 Sensors & Presence screen
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Poly Lens Cloud has a policy that locks 'Wear Detection' to 'Enabled' (ON).
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name to open Device screen.
3. Tap 'Sensors & Presence' from the settings list.
4. Observe the 'Wear Detection' toggle/switch on the Sensors & Presence screen.
5. Note the displayed value (ON or OFF).
6. Verify the displayed value matches the policy-enforced value from Poly Lens Cloud (Expected: ON).
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Wear Detection' setting displays 'ON' (Enabled), matching the locked policy value from Poly Lens Cloud. The toggle should appear in the ON position.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-002: Verify locked policy setting is disabled/read-only and cannot be changed by user
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is connected. Poly Lens Cloud has a policy locking 'Wear Detection' to 'Enabled' (ON).
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Observe the 'Wear Detection' toggle.
3. Attempt to tap the toggle to change it from ON to OFF.
4. Observe the toggle behavior — does it respond to the tap?
5. Attempt to tap and hold/drag the toggle if it is a slider-style switch.
6. Navigate away from Sensors & Presence screen (tap Back), then return to Sensors & Presence.
7. Observe the 'Wear Detection' value again.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Wear Detection' toggle is visually disabled/grayed out and does NOT respond to user taps. The value remains ON (policy-enforced). After navigating away and back, the value is still ON and still disabled.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-003: Verify multiple Sensors & Presence settings are locked simultaneously by cloud policy
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Poly Lens Cloud policy locks: 'Wear Detection' = ON, 'Auto Pause' = OFF, 'Headset Detection' = ON.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Observe all three settings: 'Wear Detection', 'Auto Pause', 'Headset Detection'.
3. For each setting, verify the displayed value matches the policy-enforced value.
4. For each setting, attempt to change the value by tapping the toggle.
5. For each setting, verify the toggle is disabled/grayed out and does not change.
generated_testcases.expected: All three Sensors & Presence settings display the correct policy-enforced values (Wear Detection=ON, Auto Pause=OFF, Headset Detection=ON). All toggles are disabled/read-only and cannot be changed by user interaction.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-004: Verify cloud policy applied while app is already connected — real-time sync/refresh
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. No policy initially applied (settings are user-controllable).
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Verify 'Wear Detection' toggle is enabled (user can change it). Set it to OFF.
3. While staying on the Sensors & Presence screen, have an administrator apply a Poly Lens Cloud policy locking 'Wear Detection' to ON.
4. Wait up to 30 seconds for policy sync.
5. Observe the 'Wear Detection' toggle on screen — does it automatically update to ON?
6. Attempt to tap the toggle to change it.
7. Navigate away (Back) and return to Sensors & Presence to verify persistence of locked state.
generated_testcases.expected: Within a reasonable sync time (≤30 seconds), the 'Wear Detection' toggle automatically updates to ON and becomes disabled/grayed out. The user cannot change it. After navigating away and back, the locked state persists.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-005: Verify locked policy state persists after app backgrounding and foregrounding
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Poly Lens Cloud policy locks 'Wear Detection' to ON.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Verify 'Wear Detection' shows ON and is disabled.
3. Press the Home button to background the app (do not kill it).
4. Wait 10 seconds.
5. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list.
6. Navigate to Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
7. Observe the 'Wear Detection' value and enabled/disabled state.
generated_testcases.expected: After backgrounding and foregrounding the app, the 'Wear Detection' setting still displays ON and remains disabled/grayed out. The locked policy state is preserved across app lifecycle transitions.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-006: Verify locked policy state persists after app force-kill and relaunch
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Poly Lens Cloud policy locks 'Wear Detection' to ON.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Verify 'Wear Detection' shows ON and is disabled.
3. Fully close/kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps or force stop).
4. Wait 5 seconds.
5. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
6. Wait for the app to connect to Voyager Free 20.
7. Navigate to Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
8. Observe the 'Wear Detection' value and enabled/disabled state.
generated_testcases.expected: After force-kill and relaunch, the app re-syncs with the cloud policy. The 'Wear Detection' setting displays ON and is disabled/grayed out. The locked policy is re-applied on reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-007: Verify locked policy state persists after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Poly Lens Cloud policy locks 'Wear Detection' to ON.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Verify 'Wear Detection' shows ON and is disabled.
3. Disconnect Bluetooth (turn off Bluetooth on phone, or put earbuds in case and close lid).
4. Wait for app to show 'Disconnected' state.
5. Reconnect Bluetooth (turn Bluetooth back on, or open case lid and wait for reconnection).
6. Wait for Poly Studio Mobile app to reconnect to Voyager Free 20.
7. Navigate to Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
8. Observe the 'Wear Detection' value and enabled/disabled state.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect, the app re-syncs with the cloud policy. The 'Wear Detection' setting displays ON and is disabled/grayed out. The locked policy is re-applied on reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-008: Verify locked policy state persists after device power off and on
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Poly Lens Cloud policy locks 'Wear Detection' to ON.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Verify 'Wear Detection' shows ON and is disabled.
3. Power off the Voyager Free 20 earbuds (place in case and close lid, or let battery die).
4. Wait for app to show 'Disconnected' state.
5. Power on the earbuds (open case lid, remove from case).
6. Wait for automatic reconnection to Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Navigate to Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
8. Observe the 'Wear Detection' value and enabled/disabled state.
generated_testcases.expected: After device power off and on, the app reconnects and re-syncs with the cloud policy. The 'Wear Detection' setting displays ON and is disabled/grayed out.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-009: Verify locked policy value overrides any locally saved user preference
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. No policy initially applied.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Set 'Wear Detection' to OFF (user preference).
3. Navigate away and back to confirm OFF is saved locally.
4. Have an administrator apply a Poly Lens Cloud policy locking 'Wear Detection' to ON.
5. Wait for policy sync (up to 30 seconds).
6. Observe the 'Wear Detection' value on the Sensors & Presence screen.
7. Verify it now shows ON (overriding the user's previous OFF setting).
8. Verify the toggle is disabled/grayed out.
generated_testcases.expected: The cloud policy value (ON) overrides the user's locally saved preference (OFF). The UI displays ON and the toggle is disabled. The user cannot revert to their previous setting.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-010: Verify policy lock indicator/icon is displayed on locked settings
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Poly Lens Cloud policy locks 'Wear Detection' to ON.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence.
2. Observe the 'Wear Detection' row/toggle.
3. Check for any visual indicator that the setting is managed by an organization/policy (e.g., lock icon, 'Managed by your organization' text, grayed-out state with info icon).
4. Compare with an unlocked setting (e.g., if 'Auto Pause' is not locked by policy, it should NOT show the lock indicator).
5. Tap on any lock indicator or info icon if present to see if additional information is displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Wear Detection' setting displays a clear visual indicator (lock icon, 'Managed' badge, or similar) showing it is enforced by a policy. Unlocked settings do not show this indicator. Tapping the indicator may show a tooltip or dialog explaining the setting is managed by IT.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-011: Verify policy lock is removed when cloud policy is deleted — setting returns to user-controllable state
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected. Poly Lens Cloud policy locks 'Wear Detection' to ON. User has verified the setting is locked and disabled.
1. Have an administrator remove/delete the policy locking 'Wear Detection' from Poly Lens Cloud.
2. Wait for policy sync (up to 30 seconds).
3. On the Poly Studio Mobile app, navigate to Voyager Free 20 → Sensors & Presence (or refresh if already on screen).
4. Observe the 'Wear Detection' toggle.
5. Verify the toggle is now enabled (not grayed out) and user can interact with it.
6. Tap the toggle to change the value to OFF.
7. Navigate away and back to confirm the change persisted.
generated_testcases.expected: After the policy is removed, the 'Wear Detection' toggle becomes enabled and user-controllable. The user can change the value and the change persists across navigation. The lock indicator is no longer displayed.

generated_testcases.title: TC-POL-SP-012: Verify Sensors & Presence screen loads correctly on Voyager Free 20 with all UI elements present
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. No policy applied.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name.
3. Tap 'Sensors & Presence' from the settings list.
4. Observe all UI elements on the screen.
5. Verify the screen title displays 'Sensors & Presence'.
6. Verify all expected settings are present: 'Wear Detection', 'Auto Pause', 'Headset Detection' (or whatever settings are applicable for Voyager Free 20).
7. Verify each setting has a label and a toggle/switch.
8. Verify toggles are interactive (can be toggled ON and OFF).
9. Verify changes persist by navigating away and back.
generated_testcases.expected: The Sensors & Presence screen loads with the correct title. All expected settings for Voyager Free 20 are displayed with labels and functional toggles. Changes persist across navigation.
